steps.title: Identify the hypothesis's directional prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis uses Hadza (70 sounds, near Africa) versus Arapaho (16 sounds, far from Africa) to illustrate its core claim: as humans spread away from Africa, each new language loses sounds. Sound inventory correlates with geographic distance from Africa—closer languages should have more sounds, farther languages fewer.

steps.title: Find the choice confirming this geographic correlation
steps.content:
B directly confirms the hypothesis by showing that the region closer to Africa (Eastern Europe) has more sounds than the farther region (East Asia). The other three contradict this relationship.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
